author: Jordan (Proxy Interface Agent)
description: Mantis is the observation and ingestion layer of the RIO system. It watches all intent sources (UI, email, API, voice, webhooks), translates raw signals into structured intent envelopes conforming to intake-schema.json, and routes them to the Governor for evaluation. Mantis sees everything but controls nothing.

role_in_three_power_separation.principle: Mantis is the Observer in the Three-Power Separation architecture. It translates goals into structured intents but cannot approve, deny, or execute any action. The authorization field is always null on intake — only the Governor can fill it.
role_in_three_power_separation.boundary: Mantis operates upstream of the Governor. It has no access to execution tokens, approval signatures, or the kill switch. It cannot modify policies or alter ledger entries.

capabilities: Receive raw goals from any registered intent source
Translate natural language into structured intent envelopes
Validate identity (WHO), intent (WHAT), and context (WHY) fields
Assign risk_scope and urgency classification based on content analysis
Route validated intents to the Governor via POST /intent
Monitor signal patterns for the Learning Loop (read-only)
Detect duplicate or conflicting intents before submission
prohibitions: Cannot approve or deny any action
Cannot fill or alter the authorization field
Cannot access execution tokens or approval signatures
Cannot write to the ledger directly

intent_sources.description: ONE App interface — user submits intent through the proxy dashboard or approval screen
Outlook/Gmail with subject 'RIO Intent' — email body contains JSON intent envelope
intent_sources.protocol: Power Automate → Azure Service Bus → Observer → POST /intent

intent_sources.description: Direct API submission from authorized agents or external systems
Incoming webhooks from connected services (GitHub, Slack, Calendar events)
Voice-to-intent via speech transcription and natural language parsing
intent_sources.protocol: Audio → Whisper transcription → intent extraction → POST /intent

required_fields.authorization: null (always null on intake — filled by Governor)

enforcement_gates.check: Is the agent or human recognized in the registered_agents list?
Does the intake conform to intake-schema.json?
Has an identical intent been submitted within the dedup window?
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: WARN — flag as potential duplicate, allow with annotation

api_contracts.description: Submit a structured intent envelope. Mantis validates and routes to Governor.
api_contracts.request_body: intake-schema.json envelope with identity, intent, context, authorization=null

api_contracts.description: List all submitted intents with their current governance status.
api_contracts.response: Array of intent objects with status, timestamps, and governance decisions

api_contracts.description: Retrieve a specific intent by ID with full audit trail.
api_contracts.response: Complete intent object including governance decision, receipt (if executed), and ledger position

pattern_monitoring.description: Mantis passively monitors intent patterns to feed the Learning Loop. This is read-only observation — Mantis cannot modify policies based on patterns.
pattern_monitoring.output: Pattern reports available via GET /api/sync for the ProxyDashboard

relationship_to_other_components.governor: Mantis routes validated intents to the Governor. The Governor evaluates policy and returns a governance decision. Mantis has no influence on the decision.
relationship_to_other_components.executor: Mantis has no direct relationship to the Executor. Intents flow Observer → Governor → Executor. Mantis never communicates with the Executor.
relationship_to_other_components.ledger: Mantis does not write to the ledger. The Executor writes receipts after execution. Mantis can read ledger entries for pattern monitoring (read-only).
relationship_to_other_components.learning_loop: Mantis feeds pattern data to the Learning Loop, which proposes policy updates. Proposed updates go through governance before activation.

invariants: Every intent must have Identity, Intent, and Context before routing to Governor
Authorization is always null on intake — Mantis cannot pre-approve
Mantis cannot execute any external action
All rejected intents are logged with reason codes
Pattern monitoring is read-only — Mantis cannot modify policies
Mantis cannot communicate directly with the Executor
